mnemonics: Orthonormalize Your Vectors: Subtract Their Shadows (Projections) Step-by-Step
keyPoints: The process takes a linearly independent set and produces an orthonormal set that spans the same subspace.
Each new vector is created by subtracting the projection of the current vector onto all previously created orthonormal vectors.
The resulting set is orthonormal, meaning all vectors are unit vectors and mutually perpendicular.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to normalize the final vectors (making them unit length).
Applying the process to a linearly dependent set, which will result in a zero vector at some step.
